intent: Produce a Comparison report on the three fusion reactor designs of Tokamak, Stellarator, and Laser Inertial Fusion, across five specific use cases within the field of fusion energy research. The report must be derived from a pool of at least 120 distinct sandbox URLs, with 60 cited as markdown links, sourced from the following: __SHOPPING__ (products: >= 12 with price, rating, review_count, feature_claim), __REDDIT__ (threads: >= 30 from forums such as r/Physics, r/Space, r/engineering), and __WIKIPEDIA__ (articles: mandatory - Tokamak, Stellarator, Inertial confinement fusion, Laser inertial fusion, Plasma physics, Magnetic confinement, ITER, National Ignition Facility, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, Fusion power plant, plus 15 more). Format every fact as markdown links `[label](url)`, using only sandbox-local URLs and no fabrication. Ensure synthesis requirements are met: (A) Matrix with exactly 3 reactor paths x 5 use cases (best, acceptable, poor) rated, (B) Path persona paragraphs (3) explaining which reactor suits specific user profiles, (C) At least 5 Reddit threads per persona. Do NOT create a TOP-10 list; this is a comparison, not ranking.
author_notes: Deep-tier task generated by gen_task_spec.py from configs/deep_topics/fusion_reactor_comparison.yaml. Golden: must=140, pool=670.
